name: Digger Terraform GitOps API
what_it_does: Open-source Terraform GitOps orchestration tool with REST API — agents manage Terraform plan/apply workflows through pull requests, with drift detection, policy gates, and team approval workflows running in existing CI (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, etc.).
use_cases: Triggering Terraform plans and applies via PR-based GitOps workflow automation
Querying infrastructure drift detection results for compliance monitoring agents
Managing team approval workflows for infrastructure changes programmatically
Integrating Terraform workflow status into ChatOps and incident response pipelines
Orchestrating multi-project Terraform deployments with dependency ordering
not_for: Teams without GitHub/GitLab for CI — Digger requires Git-based CI integration
Non-Terraform IaC (use Pulumi or Crossplane tools for those)
Organizations needing managed Terraform service without self-hosting (use Spacelift)
best_when: A team needs open-source Terraform GitOps without paying for Terraform Cloud or Spacelift, using existing GitHub Actions infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need a fully-managed Terraform service with enterprise SLAs — use Terraform Cloud, Spacelift, or env0.

auth.notes: API token from Digger cloud dashboard. Self-hosted option uses local authentication. GitHub App integration for PR-based workflow. Organization-scoped tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free to self-host; Cloud: free tier for small teams
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully self-hostable. Cloud option adds UI, drift detection, and team management. Unique open-source positioning vs commercial Terraform GitOps tools.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: unlimited; cloud: rate limits per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation covers self-hosting and API basics. Terraform plan/apply status available via API. Webhook delivery for workflow events. Error responses are JSON.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Terraform plan triggers are idempotent (same state). Apply operations are not — duplicate applies may cause infrastructure conflicts. Drift checks are idempotent reads.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires GitHub App or GitLab integration setup before API use
Terraform state backend must be configured separately — Digger orchestrates, doesn't store state
Plan/apply latency depends on CI runner availability — not API latency
Self-hosted and cloud versions have feature differences — verify capability before building

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — full audit possible. Self-hosted means full data control. Cloud SOC2 compliant. GitHub App integration uses least-privilege scopes. Terraform state stored in user's own backend.
